# operating-instructions-override.md
<!-- This file is part of the intent package. It is read by the Manager and
     composed into the run-level operating-instructions.md for the Developer.

     Usually empty. Only create overrides when this intent genuinely needs
     different policy than meta/global-operating-instructions.md.

     If unused, leave the Overrides section as: None -->

Owner: <n>
Last Updated: YYYY-MM-DD

---

## Rules
<!-- Do not remove these — they apply to every intent regardless of overrides. -->

- This file is **policy only**. No task lists, no scope, no deliverables.
- It becomes immutable once execution begins.
- Overrides apply to this intent only — they do not change global policy.
- If a rule here conflicts with global operating instructions, this file wins.

---

## What belongs here
<!-- Valid overrides include:
     - Output format or language requirements (e.g., "all output in British English")
     - Tone or communication style constraints
     - Model behavior rules specific to this intent (e.g., "do not suggest alternatives")
     - Confidentiality or sensitivity handling (e.g., "do not log PII")
     - Tool or platform restrictions beyond what global instructions specify
     - Any policy the Architect needs enforced that global instructions don't cover -->

## What does not belong here
<!-- - Tasks or work items → those go in tasks.md
     - Scope or deliverables → those go in intent.md
     - File locations or inputs → those go in intent.md Dependencies & Files
     - Anything the Developer needs to *do* rather than *follow* -->

---

## Overrides

None
